message:
My sister calls me 'the emergency kit person' — and honestly? I've leaned into it. Last week our power went out for 18 hours, and while the rest of the block was scrambling, I was over here with my flashlights, my backup phone charger, and yes, my water bottles. The kids thought it was basically camping. I caught myself actually *enjoying* the power outage because we were... ready.

It's funny how that works. Being prepared doesn't mean you're paranoid — it just means you're free to roll with whatever happens without that low hum of worry in the background.

I'm curious: do you feel more calm when you know your family has the basics covered, or is it still something you're working on?
